what: Optimize NMF with kernel covariates (Full Support for Missing Values) — nmfkc
title: Optimize NMF with kernel covariates (Full Support for Missing Values) — nmfkc
text: nmfkc fits nonnegative matrix factorization kernel covariates tri-factorization model \(Y \approx X C = X B\). function supports two major input modes: Matrix Mode (Existing): nmfkc(Y=matrix, =matrix, ...) Formula Mode (New): nmfkc(formula=Y_vars ~ A_vars, data=df, rank=Q, ...) rank basis matrix can specified using either rank argument (preferred formula mode) hidden Q argument (backward compatibility).

title: Optimize NMF with kernel covariates (Full Support for Missing Values) — nmfkc
code: nmfkc(Y, A = NULL, rank = NULL, data, epsilon = 1e-04, maxit = 5000, ...)

title: Optimize NMF with kernel covariates (Full Support for Missing Values) — nmfkc
text: Y Observation matrix (P x N), formula object Formula Mode. Formula Mode, use Y1 + Y2 ~ A1 + A2 data, Y_matrix ~ A_matrix direct matrix evaluation. Supports dot notation (. ~ A1 + A2) data supplied. Covariate matrix. Default NULL (covariates). Ignored Y formula. rank Integer. rank basis matrix \(X\) (Q). Preferred Q. data Optional. data frame variables formula taken. epsilon Positive convergence tolerance. maxit Maximum number iterations. ... Additional arguments passed fine-tuning regularization, initialization, constraints, output control. includes backward-compatible arguments Q method. Y.weights: Optional numeric matrix (P x N) vector (length N). 0 indicates missing/ignored values. NULL (default), weights automatically set 0 NAs Y, 1 otherwise. X.L2.ortho: Nonnegative penalty parameter orthogonality \(X\) (default: 0). minimizes -diagonal elements Gram matrix \(X^\top X\), reducing correlation basis vectors (conceptually minimizing \(\| X^\top X - \mathrm{diag}(X^\top X) \|_F^2\)). (Formerly lambda.ortho). B.L1: Nonnegative penalty parameter L1 regularization \(B = C \) (default: 0). Promotes sparsity coefficients. (Formerly gamma). C.L1: Nonnegative penalty parameter L1 regularization \(C\) (default: 0). Promotes sparsity parameter matrix. (Formerly lambda). Q: Backward-compatible name rank basis matrix (Q). method: Objective function: Euclidean distance "EU" (default) Kullback–Leibler divergence "KL". X.restriction: Constraint columns \(X\). Options: "colSums" (default), "colSqSums", "totalSum", "none", "fixed". "none" applies normalization \(X\) update, allowing absorb scale freely. automatically set Y.symmetric = "bi" "tri", column normalization prevent \(X X^\top\) (\(X C X^\top\)) approximating \(Y\) correct scale. X.init: Method initializing basis matrix \(X\). Options: "kmeans" (default), "kmeansar", "runif", "nndsvd", user-specified matrix. "kmeansar" applies \(k\)-means initialization fills zero entries Uniform(0, mean(Y)/100), analogous NNDSVDar. nstart: Number random starts kmeans initializing \(X\) (default: 1). seed: Integer seed reproducibility (default: 123). C.init: Optional numeric matrix giving initial value parameter matrix \(C\) (.e., \(\Theta\)). NULL, C dimension \(Q \times N\) (equivalently \(B\)); otherwise, C dimension \(Q \times K\) \(K = nrow()\). Default initializes entries 1. Y.symmetric: Character string specifying type symmetric NMF. "none" (default): standard NMF (\(Y \approx XB\)). "bi": 2-factor symmetric NMF (\(Y \approx X X^\top\)). Internally implemented "tri" model \(C = I_Q\) (identity matrix) held fixed, \(X\) updated freely without column normalization. multiplicative update \(X\) uses cube-root damping (\(X \leftarrow X \circ (numerator / denominator)^{1/3}\)) prevent oscillation, since \(X\) appears factors decomposition (et al., 2011, Proposition 1). "tri": 3-factor symmetric NMF (\(Y \approx X C X^\top\)) \(C\) \(Q \times Q\) matrix representing cluster interactions (Ding et al., 2006). "bi" "tri" require Y square used covariate matrix . Y.symmetric = "bi", X.restriction automatically set "none" (column normalization), \(C = I_Q\) fixed absorb scale. "tri", column normalization retained (default "colSums") free parameter matrix \(C\) absorbs scale. prefix: Prefix column names \(X\) row names \(B\) (default: "Basis"). print.trace: Logical. TRUE, prints progress every 10 iterations (default: FALSE). print.dims: Logical. TRUE (default), prints matrix dimensions elapsed time. detail: Level post-fit criterion computation. "full" computes criteria including silhouette, CPCC, dist.cor; "fast" skips expensive distance-based criteria; "minimal" returns information criteria. Default "full". backward compatibility, save.time = TRUE maps "fast" save.memory = TRUE maps "minimal".

code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("ksatohds/nmfkc") # Example 1. Matrix Mode (Existing) library(nmfkc) X <- cbind(c(1,0,1),c(0,1,0)) B <- cbind(c(1,0),c(0,1),c(1,1)) Y <- X %*% B rownames(Y) <- paste0("P",1:nrow(Y)) colnames(Y) <- paste0("N",1:ncol(Y)) print(X); print(B); print(Y) #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    1    0 #> [2,]    0    1 #> [3,]    1    0 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    1    0    1 #> [2,]    0    1    1 #>    N1 N2 N3 #> P1  1  0  1 #> P2  0  1  1 #> P3  1  0  1 library(nmfkc) res <- nmfkc(Y,Q=2,epsilon=1e-6) #> Y(3,3)~X(3,2)B(2,3)... #> 0sec res$X #>    Basis1      Basis2 #> P1      0 0.498047869 #> P2      1 0.003904261 #> P3      0 0.498047869 res$B #>              N1           N2        N3 #> Basis1 0.000000 0.9999995176 0.9920988 #> Basis2 2.007838 0.0001206861 2.0079012  # Example 2. Formula Mode set.seed(1) dummy_data <- data.frame(Y1=rpois(10,5), Y2=rpois(10,10),                          A1=abs(rnorm(10,5)), A2=abs(rnorm(10,3))) res_f <- nmfkc(Y1 + Y2 ~ A1 + A2, data=dummy_data, rank=2) #> A created using columns: A1, A2 #> Y(2,10)~X(2,2)C(2,2)A(2,10)=XB(2,10)... #> 0sec  # Example 3. Symmetric NMF (bi: Y ~ X X^T) S <- matrix(c(3,0,2, 0,3,1, 2,1,2), nrow=3) res_bi <- nmfkc(S, Q=2, Y.symmetric="bi") #> Y(3,3)~X(3,2)B(2,3)... #> 0sec res_bi$X   # basis matrix (no column normalization) #>         Basis1     Basis2 #> [1,] 0.0000000 1.71131965 #> [2,] 1.7274149 0.01105598 #> [3,] 0.5977175 1.22664897 res_bi$XB  # reconstruction X %*% t(X) #>            [,1]       [,2]     [,3] #> [1,] 2.92861495 0.01892032 2.099188 #> [2,] 0.01892032 2.98408448 1.046068 #> [3,] 2.09918848 1.04606801 1.861934  # Example 4. what: Statistical inference for the parameter matrix C (Theta) — nmfkc.inference
title: Statistical inference for the parameter matrix C (Theta) — nmfkc.inference
text: nmfkc.inference performs statistical inference parameter matrix \(C\) (\(\Theta\)) fitted nmfkc model, conditional estimated basis matrix \(\hat{X}\). working model \(Y = X C + \varepsilon\) \(\varepsilon_{pn} \stackrel{iid}{\sim} N(0, \sigma^2)\), inference conducted via sandwich covariance estimation one-step wild bootstrap non-negative projection.

Set FALSE skip bootstrap (faster, sandwich SE computed). ... Additional arguments: wild.B Number bootstrap replicates. Default 1000. wild.seed Seed bootstrap. what: Estimate Gaussian/RBF kernel parameter beta from covariates (supports landmarks) — nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med
title: Estimate Gaussian/RBF kernel parameter beta from covariates (supports landmarks) — nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med
text: Computes data-driven reference scale Gaussian/RBF kernel covariates using robust "median nearest-neighbor (nearest-landmark) distance" heuristic, returns corresponding kernel parameter \(\beta\). Gaussian/RBF kernel assumed written form $$k(u,v) = \exp\{-\beta \|u-v\|^2\} = \exp\{-\|u-v\|^2/(2\sigma^2)\},$$ hence \(\beta = 1/(2\sigma^2)\). function first estimates typical distance scale \(\sigma_0\) median distances, sets \(\beta_0 = 1/(2\sigma_0^2)\). Uk NULL, \(\sigma_0\) estimated median nearest-neighbor distances within U (excluding self-distance). Uk provided, \(\sigma_0\) estimated median nearest-landmark distances sample U closest landmark Uk. control memory usage large N (M), distances computed blocks. Optionally, columns U can randomly subsampled via sample.size reduce cost.

title: Estimate Gaussian/RBF kernel parameter beta from covariates (supports landmarks) — nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med

title: Estimate Gaussian/RBF kernel parameter beta from covariates (supports landmarks) — nmfkc.kernel.beta.nearest.med
text: Candidate grid: Along beta, function returns beta_candidates, small logarithmic grid suitable cross-validation. landmark case (Uk provided), grid designed symmetric bandwidth scale \(\sigma\) around \(\sigma_0\) one decade: $$\sigma = \sigma_0 \times 10^{t}, \quad t \\{-1,-2/3,-1/3,0,1/3,2/3,1\}.$$ Using \(\beta = 1/(2\sigma^2)\), corresponds $$\beta = \beta_0 \times 10^{-2t}.$$ Uk NULL, shorter coarse grid may returned (see Value). Notes: Uk identical U, function detects case excludes self-distances (distance 0) avoid \(\sigma_0=0\). sample.size performs random subsampling without setting seed. reproducible results, set set.seed() calling function.

what: Create a Gaussian kernel matrix from covariates — nmfkc.kernel.gaussian
title: Create a Gaussian kernel matrix from covariates — nmfkc.kernel.gaussian
text: nmfkc.kernel.gaussian constructs Gaussian (RBF) kernel matrix covariate matrices. kernel defined \(K(u,v) = \exp(-\beta \|u - v\|^2)\). V contains NA values, two methods available via na.method: "pds" Partial Distance Strategy. Computes kernel using observed (non-NA) rows, beta adjusted \(\beta_{adj} = \beta \times K / K_{obs}\) \(K\) total number rows \(K_{obs}\) number observed rows. "egk" Expected Gaussian Kernel (Mesquita et al., 2019). Uses Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) estimate conditional distribution missing values given observed values, computes expected kernel value via Gamma approximation. Requires gmm.means, gmm.sigmas, gmm.weights passed ....

what: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects — nmfre
title: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects — nmfre
text: Estimates NMF-RE model $$Y = X(\Theta + U) + \mathcal{E}$$ \(Y\) (\(P \times N\)) non-negative observation matrix, \(X\) (\(P \times Q\)) non-negative basis matrix learned data, \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)) non-negative coefficient matrix capturing systematic covariate effects latent scores, \(\) (\(K \times N\)) covariate matrix, \(U\) (\(Q \times N\)) random effects matrix capturing unit-specific deviations latent score space. NMF-RE can viewed mixed-effects latent-variable model defined reconstruction (mean) structure. non-negativity constraint \(X\) induces sparse, parts-based loadings, achieving measurement-side variable selection without explicit sparsity penalty. Inference \(\Theta\) provides covariate-side variable selection identifying covariates significantly affect components. Estimation alternates ridge-type BLUP-like closed-form updates \(U\) multiplicative non-negative updates \(X\) \(\Theta\). effective degrees freedom consumed \(U\) monitored df-based cap can enforced prevent near-saturated fits. wild.bootstrap = TRUE, inference \(\Theta\) performed conditional \((\hat{X}, \hat{U})\) via asymptotic linearization, one-step Newton update, multiplier (wild) bootstrap, yielding standard errors, z-values, p-values, confidence intervals without repeated constrained re-optimization.

title: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects — nmfre
code: nmfre(   Y,   A = NULL,   rank = 2,   df.rate = NULL,   wild.bootstrap = TRUE,   epsilon = 1e-05,   maxit = 50000,   ... )

title: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects — nmfre
text: Y Observation matrix (P x N), non-negative. Covariate matrix (K x N). Default row ones (intercept ). rank Integer. Rank basis matrix \(X\). Default 2. backward compatibility, Q accepted via .... df.rate Rate computing dfU cap (cap = rate * N * Q). backward compatibility, dfU.cap.rate accepted via .... NULL (default), runs nmfre.dfU.scan internally selects minimum rate cap binding. Use nmfre.dfU.scan beforehand examine dfU behavior across rates choose appropriate value. wild.bootstrap Logical. TRUE (default), perform wild bootstrap inference \(\Theta\). epsilon Convergence tolerance relative change objective (default 1e-5). maxit Maximum number iterations (default 50000). ... Additional arguments initialization, variance control, dfU control, optimization, inference settings. X.init: Initial basis matrix (P x Q), NULL. NULL, nmfkc called internally generate initial values. C.init: Initial coefficient matrix (Q x K), NULL. NULL, nmfkc called internally generate initial values. U.init: Initial random effects matrix (Q x N), NULL (zeros). prefix: Prefix basis names (default "Basis"). sigma2: Initial residual variance (default 1). sigma2.update: Logical. Update \(\sigma^2\) iterations (default TRUE). tau2: Initial random effect variance (default 1). tau2.update: Logical. Update \(\tau^2\) moment matching (default TRUE). Disabled dfU cap active. dfU.control: Either "cap" (default) enforce cap dfU, "" cap. print.trace: Logical. TRUE, print progress every 100 iterations (default FALSE). seed: Integer seed reproducibility (default 1). C.p.side: P-value sidedness: "one.sided" (default, boundary null H0: C=0 vs H1: C>0) "two.sided". wild.B: Number wild bootstrap replicates (default 500). wild.seed: Seed wild bootstrap (default 123).

title: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects — nmfre
text: list class "nmfre" components. model \(Y = X(\Theta + U) + \mathcal{E}\). Core matrices X Basis matrix \(X\) (\(P \times Q\)), columns normalized sum 1. C Coefficient matrix \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)). U Random effects matrix \(U\) (\(Q \times N\)). Variance components sigma2 Residual variance \(\hat{\sigma}^2\). tau2 Random effect variance \(\hat{\tau}^2\). lambda Ridge penalty \(\lambda = \sigma^2 / \tau^2\). Convergence diagnostics converged Logical. Whether algorithm converged. stop.reason Character string describing iteration stopped. iter Number iterations performed. maxit Maximum iterations setting used. epsilon Convergence tolerance used. objfunc Final objective function value \(\|Y - X(\Theta + U)\|^2 + \lambda \|U\|^2\). rel.change.final Final relative change objective. objfunc.iter Numeric vector objective values per iteration. rss.trace Numeric vector \(\|Y - X(\Theta + U)\|^2\) per iteration. Effective degrees freedom (dfU) diagnostics dfU Final effective degrees freedom \(\mathrm{df}_U = N \sum_q d_q / (d_q + \lambda)\), \(d_q\) eigenvalues \(X'X\). dfU.cap Upper bound imposed \(\mathrm{df}_U\). dfU.cap.rate Rate used compute cap. dfU.cap.scan Result nmfre.dfU.scan, NULL. lambda.enforced Final \(\lambda\) enforced satisfy cap. dfU.hit.cap Logical. Whether cap binding. dfU.hit.iter Iteration cap first bound. dfU.frac \(\mathrm{df}_U / (NQ)\), fraction maximum df. dfU.cap.frac \(\mathrm{df}_U^{\mathrm{cap}} / (NQ)\). Fitted matrices B Fixed-effect scores \(\Theta \) (\(Q \times N\)). B.prob Column-normalized probabilities \(\max(\Theta , 0)\). B.blup BLUP scores \(\Theta + U\) (\(Q \times N\)). B.blup.pos Non-negative BLUP scores \(\max(\Theta + U, 0)\) (\(Q \times N\)). B.blup.prob Column-normalized probabilities \(\max(\Theta + U, 0)\). XB Fitted values fixed effects \(X \Theta \) (\(P \times N\)). XB.blup Fitted values including random effects \(X(\Theta + U)\) (\(P \times N\)). Fit statistics r.squared Coefficient determination \(R^2\) \(Y\) vs \(X(\Theta + U)\) (BLUP). r.squared.fixed Coefficient determination \(R^2\) \(Y\) vs \(X \Theta \) (fixed effects ). ICC Trace-based Intraclass Correlation Coefficient. NMF-RE model, conditional covariance \(n\)-th observation column \(\mathrm{Var}(Y_n) = \tau^2 X X^\top + \sigma^2 I_P\), \(P \times P\) matrix. Unlike standard random intercept model design matrix \(Z\) simple indicator (ICC reduces \(\tau^2 / (\sigma^2 + \tau^2)\)), basis matrix \(X\) plays role \(Z\) random slopes model, making variance contribution \(U\) depend \(X\). obtain scalar summary, take trace component: $$\mathrm{ICC} = \frac{\tau^2 \, \mathrm{tr}(X^\top X)}       {\tau^2 \, \mathrm{tr}(X^\top X) + \sigma^2 P}.$$ equals average (\(P\) dimensions) proportion per-column variance attributable random effects. Inference \(\Theta\) (wild bootstrap) sigma2.used \(\hat{\sigma}^2\) used inference. C.vec.cov Variance-covariance matrix \(\mathrm{vec}(\Theta)\) (\(QK \times QK\)). C.se Standard error matrix \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)). C.se.hess Sandwich (Hessian-based) SE matrix \(\Theta\). C.se.boot Bootstrap SE matrix \(\Theta\). coefficients Data frame columns Estimate, Std. Error, z value, Pr(>|z|), confidence interval bounds element \(\Theta\). C.ci.lower Lower confidence interval matrix \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)). C.ci.upper Upper confidence interval matrix \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)). C.boot.sd Bootstrap standard deviation matrix \(\Theta\) (\(Q \times K\)). C.p.side P-value sidedness used: "one.sided" "two.sided".

what: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
text: nmfre.inference performs statistical inference coefficient matrix \(C\) (\(\Theta\)) fitted nmfre model, conditional estimated basis matrix \(\hat{X}\) random effects \(\hat{U}\). working model \(Y^* = Y - X\hat{U} \approx X C + \varepsilon\), inference conducted via sandwich covariance estimation one-step wild bootstrap non-negative projection. result compatible nmfkc.DOT visualization (pass result directly x type = "YXA").

title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
code: nmfre.inference(object, Y, A = NULL, wild.bootstrap = TRUE, ...)

title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
text: object object class "nmfre" returned nmfre. Y Observation matrix (P x N). Must match data used nmfre(). Covariate matrix (K x N). Default NULL (intercept ). wild.bootstrap Logical. TRUE (default), performs wild bootstrap confidence intervals bootstrap standard errors. ... Additional arguments: wild.B Number bootstrap replicates. Default 500. wild.seed Seed bootstrap. Default 123. wild.level Confidence level bootstrap CI. Default 0.95. C.p.side P-value type: "one.sided" (default) "two.sided". cov.ridge Ridge stabilization. Default 1e-8. print.trace Logical. Default FALSE.

title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
text: input object additional inference components: sigma2.used Estimated \(\sigma^2\) used inference. C.vec.cov Full covariance matrix \(vec(C)\). C.se Sandwich standard errors \(C\). C.se.boot Bootstrap standard errors \(C\). C.ci.lower Lower CI bounds \(C\). C.ci.upper Upper CI bounds \(C\). coefficients Data frame Basis, Covariate, Estimate, SE, BSE, z_value, p_value, CI_low, CI_high. C.p.side P-value type used.

title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
text: Satoh, K. (2026). Wild Bootstrap Inference Non-Negative Matrix Factorization Random Effects. arXiv:2603.01468. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01468

title: Statistical inference for the coefficient matrix C from NMF-RE — nmfre.inference
code: Y <- matrix(cars$dist, nrow = 1) A <- rbind(intercept = 1, speed = cars$speed) res <- nmfre(Y, A, Q = 1, wild.bootstrap = FALSE) res2 <- nmfre.inference(res, Y, A) res2$coefficients #>    Basis Covariate   Estimate        SE      BSE     z_value      p_value #> 1 Basis1 intercept 0.01426606 6.4271478 3.253820 0.002219656 4.991145e-01 #> 2 Basis1     speed 2.85065956 0.4564636 0.431663 6.245096893 2.117687e-10 #>     CI_low   CI_high #> 1 0.000000 10.405754 #> 2 2.091174  3.869986

what: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
title: Predict method for nmfae objects — predict.nmfae
text: predict.nmfae computes fitted predicted values three-layer NMF model. Without newY2, returns -sample fitted values \(X_1 \Theta X_2 Y_2\). newY2, computes --sample predictions \(X_1 \Theta X_2 \cdot \mathrm{newY2}\). type = "class", column classified row maximum predicted value (useful \(Y_1\) one-hot class matrix nmfkc.class). Y1 (actual values) provided, stored attribute plot.predict.nmfae can produce observed-vs-predicted scatter plot (type = "response") confusion matrix heatmap (type = "class").

what: Graphviz DOT Output Consolidation and Cleanup
title: nmfkc 0.5.8
text: Harmonized DOT-generating functions (nmf.sem.DOT, nmfkc.DOT, nmfkc.ar.DOT) consistent structure, naming conventions, visualization logic. Standardized node edge formatting rules, including unified cluster behavior, color schemes, edge-scaling conventions. Implemented threshold-aware coefficient labeling displayed numerical precision aligns visualization threshold, preventing misleadingly detailed labels. Removed unused redundant DOT fragments improved compatibility across Graphviz engines. Enhanced layout readability consistent indentation, node grouping, suppression isolated nodes specific visualization modes (e.g., type = "YA" nmfkc.DOT). Refactored expanded internal DOT helper functions (.nmfkc_dot_format_coef, .nmfkc_dot_digits_from_threshold, .nmfkc_dot_cluster_nodes, etc.) better maintainability uniform behavior. New Function: Implemented nmfkc.ecv() Element-wise Cross-Validation (Wold’s CV). function randomly masks elements observation matrix evaluate structural reconstruction error. provides statistically robust criterion rank selection, avoiding monotonic error decrease often seen standard column-wise CV. Supports vector input rank evaluate multiple ranks simultaneously. Missing Value & Weight Support: nmfkc() nmfkc.cv() now fully support missing values (NA) observation weights via hidden argument Y.weights (passed ...). Y contains NAs, automatically detected masked (assigned weight 0) optimization. Rank Selection Diagnostics (nmfkc.rank): Dual-Axis Visualization: plot now displays fitting metrics (R2R^2, etc.) left axis ECV Sigma (RMSE) right axis (blue line). Elbow: Geometric elbow point R2R^2 curve. Min: Minimum error point Element-wise CV. save.time defaults FALSE, enabling robust Element-wise CV calculation default. Argument Standardization: Unified rank argument name rank across functions (nmfkc, nmfkc.cv, nmfkc.ecv, nmfkc.rank). legacy argument Q still supported backward compatibility internally mapped rank. Summary Improvements: Sparsity Basis (XX) Coefficients (BB). Clustering Entropy (indicating “Crisp” vs “Ambiguous” clustering). Clustering Crispness (Mean Max Probability). Number percentage missing values YY. Improvements: Added validation check nmfkc.ar() ensure input Y missing values (propagated covariate matrix VAR models). Refined nmfkc.residual.plot() layout margins better visibility titles. Updated documentation reflect changes. Regularization Update: regularization scheme revised L2 (ridge) L1 (lasso-type) penalties. gamma now controls L1 penalty coefficient matrix ( B = C ), promoting sparsity sample-wise coefficients. new argument lambda added control L1 penalty parameter matrix ( C ), encouraging sparsity shared template structure. parameters can passed ellipsis (...) nmfkc() related functions. Function Signature Simplification:** Many less-frequently used arguments nmfkc() (e.g., gamma, X.restriction, X.init) nmfkc.cv() (e.g., div, seed) moved ellipsis (...) cleaner function signature. Performance Improvement: internal function .silhouette.simple vectorized optimized reduce computational cost, particularly calculation () b(). Removed fast.calc option nmfkc() function. Added X.init argument nmfkc() function, allowing selection 'kmeans' 'nndsvd' initialization methods. penalty term changed tr(CC') tr(BB') = tr(CAA'C'). Implemented internal .z xnorm functions. Added fast.calc option nmfkc() function. Optimized internal calculations improved performance. Updated citation("nmfkc") added AIC/BIC output. Implemented nmfkc.ar.stationarity() function. Modified z() function. Used crossprod() faster matrix multiplication. Implemented nmfkc.ar.DOT() function. Added logic sort columns X form unit matrix special cases. Implemented nmfkc.kernel.beta.cv() nmfkc.ar.degree.cv() functions. Set default column names X Basis1, Basis2, etc. Added X.prob X.cluster return object. Skipped CPCC silhouette calculations save.time = TRUE. Added prototype nmfkc.ar() function. Added criterion argument nmfkc() function support multiple criteria. Updated nmfkc.rank() function. Added criterion argument nmfkc.rank() function. Implemented save.time argument. Implemented nmfkc.rank() function. Implemented nstart option kmeans() function. Added experimental implementation nmfkc.rank() function. Removed zero-variance columns rows warning. Added source references documentation. Renamed several components clarity: nmfkcreg nmfkc create.kernel nmfkc.kernel nmfkcreg.cv nmfkc.cv P B.prob cluster B.cluster unit X.column trace print.trace dims print.dims Added r.squared argument nmfkcreg.cv() function. nmfkcreg(): Added dims argument check matrix sizes. Added unit argument normalize basis matrix columns. Modified create.kernel() function support prediction. Updated examples GitHub. Removed YHAT return value; use XB instead. Added cluster return value hard clustering.
